articles.title: Panic At Chandni Chowk: Will PM Modi’s ‘No Gold’ Call Cripple Delhi’s Iconic Jewellery Hub?
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s appeal to halt gold purchases has sent shockwaves through India’s $80 billion gems and jewellery industry. With gold imports hitting a record $72 billion in fiscal year 2026-a 24% surge the government is prioritizing the stabilization of the rupee and the current account deficit over luxury consumption. However, the move has sparked deep anxiety among jewellers in hubs like Delhi’s Chandni Chowk and Mumbai’s Zaveri Bazaar. Industry leaders warn of a looming crisis for the lakhs of workers whose livelihoods depend on the sector, urging the government to consult stakeholders before implementing restrictive curbs.

articles.description: Prateek Yadav, son of Mulayam Singh Yadav and stepbrother of Akhilesh Yadav, passed away at 38 in Lucknow. His wife, Aparna Bisht Yadav, a former SP candidate who later joined the BJP, currently serves as vice-chairperson of the State Women Commission. Prateek had previously accused Aparna of damaging family relations.

articles.title: Kidney health in India: The food and hydration mistakes doctor wants you to avoid
articles.description: Kidneys often go unnoticed until problems arise. Dr. Harsha Mandadi Varadaraju highlights that aging kidneys don't have to decline; lifestyle choices, especially diet, are crucial. Excessive salt intake, a common issue in Indian households, significantly impacts blood pressure and kidney health. Staying hydrated is vital, particularly in regions prone to kidney stones.

The report has increased speculation around the Giants’ future before the 2026 MLB Draft.

articles.title: 'People's will through lawmakers can bring religious reforms': Supreme Court
articles.description: Constitutional courts should be extremely reluctant to alter religious practices, a nine-judge Supreme Court bench cautioned. Only the will of the people, expressed through elected representatives in Parliament and assemblies, can bring about reforms in religious customs. The court emphasized that it's impossible to scrutinize every religious practice.
